title: How to Run OpenAI Codex on Android — Complete Setup Guide | Acode
description: Run OpenAI Codex on your Android phone with Acode. Full Alpine Linux terminal, step-by-step setup, and integrated code editor. The only Android IDE that supports Codex natively.
heroHeadline: Run OpenAI Codex on Your Android Phone
heroSubheadline: Acode is the only Android development environment that lets you run OpenAI Codex natively. Set up Codex in Acode's Alpine Linux terminal and get AI-powered coding on your phone.
problemHeadline: The Problem: Codex Needs a Real Terminal
problemPoints: OpenAI Codex is a powerful command-line AI coding tool — but it runs in a terminal. It needs Node.js, npm, and a proper shell environment to function.
Android doesn't come with a terminal. Regular code editor apps can only edit text — they can't run commands, install packages, or execute CLI tools like Codex.
Even if you install a terminal app like Termux, you'd need to switch back and forth between the terminal (for Codex) and an editor (for your code). There's no integrated workflow.
For Android developers who want AI-assisted coding, there has been no solution that combines editor + terminal + Codex in one seamless experience. Until now.
solutionHeadline: Acode: The Only Android IDE with Codex Support
solutionPoints: Acode provides a native Alpine Linux terminal inside the app. This is the same environment Codex expects — bash, apk, and full package management.
Install Node.js with apk add nodejs npm and follow OpenAI's instructions to install Codex. It's the exact same setup process as on a desktop, just on your phone.

The real power is Acode's editor-terminal integration. Codex generates and modifies code in the terminal. You view and edit it in Acode's editor with syntax highlighting. Use acode . to open your project from terminal to editor instantly.
Acode gives you the complete AI coding loop on Android: ask Codex to write code, review the output, run it in the terminal, debug with Codex, and iterate — all without leaving the app.

setupSteps.title: Install Acode
setupSteps.description: Get Acode from Google Play or F-Droid. Free and open source.

setupSteps.title: Set Up the Alpine Linux Terminal
setupSteps.description: Tap the terminal icon. Acode will download and configure Alpine Linux via proot. Takes about a minute on first launch.

setupSteps.title: Install Prerequisites
setupSteps.description:
Run in terminal:
apk update && apk add nodejs npm git
This gives you Node.js, npm, and Git — everything Codex needs.

setupSteps.title: Install OpenAI Codex
setupSteps.description:
Follow OpenAI's documentation for current Codex CLI installation. Typically involves:
npm install -g @openai/codex (check OpenAI docs for exact package name). Then authenticate with your OpenAI API key.

setupSteps.title: Run Codex
setupSteps.description: Start Codex in Acode's terminal and begin coding. Codex can generate functions, explain code, refactor files, and help debug — directly on your phone.

setupSteps.title: Work with Files in Acode's Editor
setupSteps.description:
When Codex generates or modifies code, open the files in Acode's editor:
acode . (opens folder) or acode file.js (opens specific file). Full syntax highlighting for 100+ languages.

features.title: AI Code Generation on Mobile
features.description: Ask Codex to write code in any language — Python, JavaScript, Go, Rust, and more. It generates production-ready code while you're on the go.

features.title: Intelligent Code Explanation
features.description: Paste any code snippet into Codex and get a detailed explanation of what it does, how it works, and suggestions for improvement.

features.title: Automated Refactoring
features.description: Let Codex refactor your codebase — extract functions, rename variables, convert between patterns — while Acode's editor shows the results with full syntax highlighting.

features.title: Multi-Language Support
features.description: Codex works across dozens of programming languages. Acode supports over 100 languages with syntax highlighting. Together, you're never limited.

features.title: Terminal-Native Workflow
features.description: Codex runs where it performs best: in the terminal. Send commands, get output, pipe results, and chain operations — all within Acode's Linux environment.

features.title: Complete Mobile IDE
features.description: Codex + Acode's editor + Alpine Linux terminal = the closest thing to a desktop AI coding environment on Android. Git, npm, SSH — all included.

faqs.question: Is Codex free to use on Android?
faqs.answer: OpenAI Codex has its own pricing (check openai.com/pricing). Acode is completely free and open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: Can I use Codex offline on my phone?
faqs.answer: Codex requires an internet connection to communicate with OpenAI's API. Acode's editor and terminal work offline, but the AI agent needs connectivity.

faqs.question: Does Acode require root access?
faqs.answer: No. Acode's Alpine Linux terminal runs via proot — entirely in userspace. Works on any Android 7.0+ device without root.

faqs.question: Can I use Codex alongside Claude Code?
faqs.answer: Yes! Acode's terminal can run multiple AI tools. You can have Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode all installed and switch between them based on your needs.

faqs.question: Is Acode only for AI coding?
faqs.answer: No. Acode is a complete Android IDE. It includes a code editor for 100+ languages, Git integration, FTP/SFTP, SSH, live preview, JavaScript console, and 250+ plugins. AI coding is one of many features.

faqs.question: How does Acode compare to using Codex on a desktop?
faqs.answer: The Codex experience is the same — it's the same CLI tool. The difference is Acode provides the terminal and editor on your phone. You get mobile convenience without sacrificing the tool's capabilities.
